data.title: Loma Negra reports Q2 results
data.description:
• Loma Negra press release (NYSE:LOMA): Q2 net sales revenues stood at Ps. 174,511 million (US$ 149 million), and decreased by 8.0% YoY, mainly explained by a decrease of 9,9% in in the top line of the Cement segment.
  • Net Profit Attributable to Owners of the Company stood at Ps. 397 million. During the quarter, the company reported a gain per common share of Ps. 0.6809 and an ADR gain of Ps. 3.4047, compared to a gain per common share of Ps. 70.7981 and a gain per ADR of Ps. 353.9903 in 2Q24.

data.title: Kingsway Financial Services reports Q2 results
data.description:
• Kingsway Financial Services press release (NYSE:KFS): Q2 Revenue of $30.9M.
  • Consolidated net loss was $3.2 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to a net loss of $2.2 million in the prior year period.
  • Adjusted consolidated EBITDA decreased $0.8 million to $1.7 million for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to $2.5 million in the prior year period.

data.title: Silvercorp Metals Non-GAAP EPS of $0.10, revenue of $81.33M
data.description:
• Silvercorp Metals press release (NYSE:SVM): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.10.
  • Revenue of $81.33M (+12.7% Y/Y).

  • Produced approximately 1.8 million ounces ("oz") of silver, 2,050 oz of gold, or approximately 2.0 million ounces of silver equivalent1, 15.7 million pounds ("lb") of lead and 5.2 million lb of zinc;

    • Sold approximately 1.8 million oz of silver, 1,951 oz of gold, 15.2 million lb of lead, and 5.2 million lb of zinc, for revenue of $81.3 million;
    • All-in sustaining cost ("AISC") per oz of silver, net of by-product credits, of $13.49;
    • Net income attributable to equity shareholders of $18.1 million, or $0.08 per share;
    • Adjusted net income attributable to equity shareholders of $21.0 million, or $0.10 per share, after excluding a $4.8 million charge on the fair value of derivative liabilities and warrants and other non-cash or non-routine items;

data.title: Tripadvisor shares climb after Q2 earnings beat, revenue in line
data.description:
Shares of Tripadvisor (NASDAQ:TRIP) rose 3% in extended trading Thursday after the travel platform beat Wall Street’s expectations for second-quarter earnings and posted in-line revenue.

The company reported adjusted earnings of $0.46 a share, topping analysts’ consensus estimate of $0.43. Revenue for the quarter came in at $529 million, up 7% from the same period last year and essentially matching the $529.7 million analysts were expecting.

Net income for the second quarter was $36 million, or $0.28 a share, compared with $24 million, or $0.17 a share, a year earlier. Adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization rose 11% year-over-year to $107 million, representing 20.2% of revenue.

Chief Executive Matt Goldberg in a statement called the quarter “another example of our disciplined investment decisions and execution,” noting the company’s strategic shift toward higher-growth segments.

“We will continue to prioritize the highest growth opportunities across travel by leveraging our unique assets and trusted position across travel and experiences,” he said.

Segment results

Performance was mixed across Tripadvisor’s (NASDAQ:TRIP) segments. Revenue at Viator, the company’s experiences and tours platform, rose 11% to $270 million, while TheFork, its dining reservation business, posted 28% growth to $54 million. The core Brand Tripadvisor segment declined 3% to $242 million, weighed down by weaker media and advertising revenues.

Despite the brand segment’s softness, finance chief Mike Noonan emphasized the strong performance of the company’s marketplace businesses.

“We delivered solid second quarter results, with consolidated revenue meeting expectations, and adjusted ebitda exceeding expectations,” he said.

The company also reported a sharp improvement in cash flow. Free cash flow for the quarter surged to $177 million, up from $37 million a year ago, and Tripadvisor (TRIP) ended the quarter with $1.2 billion in cash and equivalents.

During the quarter, Tripadvisor (TRIP) repurchased approximately 2.8 million shares for $40 million at an average price of $14.22 a share. The company has $160 million remaining under its buyback authorization.
